title: Rubio Hosts Israel and Lebanon for Rare Meeting Shadowed by U.S.-Iran War
summary: The gathering ended with encouraging words, even as Israel continued to refuse to halt its military campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon.

title: 51 Percent of Americans Think Trump’s Military Action in Iran Has Not Been Worthwhile
summary: A survey from Ipsos and Reuters, released on Tuesday, found few Americans — 24 percent — think the war in Iran has been worth the costs and benefits.

title: US Blockade Stops Iran-Linked Ships From Crossing Strait of Hormuz
summary: U.S. Central Command said that six vessels had complied with directions to turn around and re-enter an Iranian port.

title: Israel and Lebanon Talk, but Fighting Continues
summary: Also, a Ukrainian city is bouncing back with Denmark’s help. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.

title: King Charles III and Queen Camilla Will Visit D.C., New York and Virginia
summary: The planned four-day state visit comes at a fraught time in the U.S.-U.K. relationship, following President Trump’s frequent belittling of the British prime minister.

title: Justice Dept. Moves to Vacate Jan. 6 Convictions for Far-Right Extremists
summary: Defending the convictions would likely have required administration officials to assert that far-right groups were acting on behalf of President Trump on Jan. 6, 2021.

title: Trump Administration Accuses Biden DOJ of Unfairly Prosecuting Anti-Abortion Activists
summary: The report is part of the president’s effort to claim anti-conservative and anti-Christian biases in federal law enforcement, even as he pushes to wield the legal system against his political enemies.

title: Gallego Dismissed Rumors of a ‘Flirty’ Swalwell, Highlighting a Culture of Silence
summary: The admission by Senator Ruben Gallego that he had heard, but disbelieved, rumors about Eric Swalwell and women showed the attitude on Capitol Hill toward men accused of behaving badly.

title: A New Accuser Says Eric Swalwell Sexually Assaulted Her
summary: The woman said Mr. Swalwell, who resigned from Congress on Tuesday afternoon, raped her in a West Hollywood hotel room in 2018. She said she believed she was drugged.

title: Swalwell’s Exit Injects ‘Chaos’ Into California Governor’s Race
summary: Democratic candidates see sudden voter interest in the sleepy contest as a campaign opportunity. All are scrambling for support from former backers of Eric Swalwell.

title: Resignations of Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales Set Up Special Election Fights in Texas and California
summary: Gov. Gavin Newsom of California called a special election to replace Mr. Swalwell, while Gov. Greg Abbott of Texas had yet to announce one for Mr. Gonzales’s successor.

title: Daring and Dazzling, a New LACMA Floats Above Los Angeles
summary: After $724 million and a decade of battles, the pugnacious David Geffen Galleries reassert the city’s role as a petri dish for experimental design.

title: Can ‘Michael’ Help Restore Jackson’s Image? His Estate Is Banking on It.
summary: A new biopic is the latest move in the Jackson estate’s posthumous — and lucrative — rehabilitation campaign.

title: A Police Chief Had a $4.5 Million Gambling Problem. No One Knew.
summary: New Haven’s police chief, Karl Jacobson, resigned abruptly after his deputies saw red flags, including missing money. He has pleaded not guilty to embezzling city money to gamble on sports.

title: Hampshire College Will Close Amid Student Enrollment Declines
summary: Other small private colleges like Hampshire have closed in recent years as financial pressures and competition for students increase.

title: Texas A&M Picks an Insider for President After Months of Conflict
summary: Debates over how to teach about gender, sexuality and other topics have shaken the school, and lead to the ouster of the previous president at the College Station campus last summer.

title: How Peter Magyar Defeated Viktor Orban, a Former Ally, In Hungary’s Election
summary: For years, Peter Magyar was a loyal ally of Viktor Orban, the far-right Hungarian leader. Then he changed sides — and defeated his former boss in a landslide victory on Sunday. Does he represent real change?

title: Hochul Proposes Tax on N.Y.C. Second Homes That Are Worth $5 Million
summary: Gov. Kathy Hochul, who has opposed raising taxes, favored a “pied-à-terre” luxury tax because it largely targets the ultrawealthy who primarily live outside New York City.

title: Camp Mystic Hearing in Texas Weighs Reopening After Deadly Flooding
summary: Testimony at a hearing this week has focused on what camp leaders knew and did as floodwaters rose in July, killing at least 116 people.

title: Ricky Cobb Has Built a Mini-Empire Through ’70s Nostalgia
summary: Ricky Cobb has built a big online following with his irreverent postings about the absurdities of the 1970s.

title: The $40 Half Chicken That Ruffled Brooklyn
summary: A New York City councilman’s Instagram post is just the latest entry in a fierce debate about the price of dining out.

title: How Trump Can Wrap Up the Iran War
summary: He can offer the regime a series of fundamental choices.

title: As a Tough-on-Crime Prosecutor, I Was Wrong About Harsh Sentencing
summary: I helped build a punitive criminal justice system. Decades later, this system is failing this country’s aging prisoners. We must set them free.

title: Have Mental Health Concerns Influenced Your Choice to Have Children?
summary: If you are on the fence, or have already made a decision, we want to hear about it.

title: Surgeon Who Removed Wrong Organ From Patient Is Charged in His Death
summary: Dr. Thomas Shaknovsky tried to persuade his colleagues in the operating room that the liver he removed from a 70-year-old patient was a spleen, according to Florida’s Health Department.

title: Designer Fashion Hits the 2026 WNBA Draft
summary: Fashion brands have taken note of the WNBA draft, described by Lauren Betts, the No. 4 draft pick, as the Met Gala of women’s basketball. Vanessa Friedman, our chief fashion critic, was there.

title: Powerful Storms Prompt Tornado Watches Across the Central U.S.
summary: More than 125 million people face some risk of severe weather on Tuesday, forecasters warned, amid a multiday outbreak of storms.

title: N.F.L. Reporter Resigns From The Athletic Amid an Investigation
summary: The publication, which is owned by The New York Times, was investigating the conduct of Dianna Russini after photographs showed her with the head coach of the New England Patriots.

title: Taylor Frankie Paul Will Not Face New Domestic Violence Charges
summary: Prosecutors in Utah investigated after the reality star’s ex-boyfriend told the police she had scratched, shoved and struck him during a fight in February.
